attributes.description: Alerts when a DaemonSet has misscheduled nodes (pods running where they shouldn't) or is not fully scheduled (current < desired). Indicates node selector, taint/toleration, or scheduling issues.
attributes.name: [K8s OTel] DaemonSet mis-scheduled or not ready

investigation_guide.blob:
## DaemonSet Mis-scheduled or Not Ready

### Triage Steps
1. Identify the affected DaemonSet(s) from the alert results.
2. Check the Workload Health dashboard for DaemonSet status.
3. Run `kubectl describe daemonset <name> -n <namespace>` for events and scheduling details.
4. Check node taints and DaemonSet tolerations: `kubectl get nodes -o json | jq '.items[].spec.taints'`.
5. For misscheduled pods, check if node selectors or affinity rules changed.

### Common Causes
- Node taints changed without updating DaemonSet tolerations
- Node selectors no longer match available nodes
- New nodes added without expected labels
- Resource constraints preventing pod scheduling on some nodes

### Tuning
- This rule alerts on both misscheduled (pods on wrong nodes) and not-ready (fewer ready than desired).
- If your DaemonSet intentionally runs on a subset of nodes, verify the desired count is correct.

### Escalation
DaemonSet issues affect cluster-wide services (logging, monitoring, networking). Investigate promptly.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-k8sclusterreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE k8s.daemonset.desired_scheduled_nodes IS NOT NULL
| STATS
    desired = LAST(k8s.daemonset.desired_scheduled_nodes, @timestamp),
    current = LAST(k8s.daemonset.current_scheduled_nodes, @timestamp),
    ready = LAST(k8s.daemonset.ready_nodes, @timestamp),
    misscheduled = LAST(k8s.daemonset.misscheduled_nodes, @timestamp)
    BY k8s.daemonset.name, k8s.namespace.name
// Alert when pods are misscheduled OR not all desired nodes are ready
| WHERE misscheduled > 0
    OR ready < desired
| EVAL not_ready = desired - ready
| KEEP k8s.namespace.name, k8s.daemonset.name, desired, ready, not_ready, misscheduled
